1.original: Someone hesitantly says something like, well, you might cut down crime if you established more youth centers.

2.original: Before long, the group, which ten minutes ago was solidly against the idea, now begins to work up real enthusiasm.

4.original: Most crime stems from the low income levels.

5.original: Conduct research to spot potential criminals before they commit a crime.

6.original: Develop surgical procedures to cure some kinds of criminals.

7.original: Educate law enforcement personnel in positive methods of reform.

8.original: These are just samples of the 78 specific ideas I've tabulated that could help accomplish the goal of eliminating jails.

9.original: When you believe, your mind finds ways to do.

10.original: This experiment has just one point.

11.original: When you believe something is impossible, your mind goes to work for you to prove why.

12.original: But when you believe, really believe, something can be done, your mind goes to work for you and helps you find the ways to do it.

13.original: Believing something can be done paves the way for creative solutions.

14.original: Believing something can't be done is destructive thinking.

15.original: This point applies to all situations, little and big.

16.original: The political leaders who do not genuinely believe permanent world peace can be established will fail because their minds are closed to creative ways to bring about peace.

17.original: The economists who believe business depressions are inevitable will not develop creative ways to beat the business cycle.

18.original: In a similar fashion, you can find ways to like a person if you believe you can.

19.original: You can discover solutions to personal problems if you believe you can.

20.original: You can find a way to purchase that new larger home if you believe you can.

23.original: Believe and you'll start thinking constructively.

24.original: Your mind will create a way if you let it.
